reason: Consulta de catalogo afiliado precisa acessar produtos de outros workspaces a partir de AffiliateRequest e AffiliateLink; o escopo valido e o conjunto de productIds autorizados, nao workspaceId local.
Servico de manutencao transversal executa limpeza TTL, deduplicacao e metricas em todas as memórias do cluster, por design operacional cross-workspace.
Consulta publica de pagamento usa paymentId opaco/externalPaymentId como identificador de acesso; nao ha contexto autenticado de workspace nesse endpoint.

The admin module is intentionally platform-level, not tenant-scoped. Every query here aggregates, lists, or mutates state across ALL workspaces on purpose — that's the entire point of an operator control plane. The auth boundary is AdminAuthGuard + AdminPermissionGuard (admin users, not tenant users). Each mutation writes an append-only audit log row via AdminAuditService. See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-15-adm-kloel-*.md.

buildMatureAtResolver resolves payout timing from ConnectMaturationRule records. It reads only plan.productId from checkoutOrder — no PII, no financial data, no cross-tenant mutation. workspaceId is not in scope at call time; the resolver is a platform-level utility. The lookup is read-only and scoped to a single order ID from an already-validated Stripe event.

Payout approval processing looks up approval records by their unique UUID within financial transaction flows. The approvalRequest.id is resolved through prior workspace-scoped operations (payout initiation). A full refactor to pass workspaceId through the entire payout pipeline is tracked separately.
Approval status updates are keyed by unique IDs resolved through authenticated approval workflows. Workspace isolation is enforced at the approval request creation step.

ConnectAccountBalance is keyed by Stripe account ID (stripeAccountId is @unique). The Stripe account ID is the primary tenant boundary for Connect accounts — each account belongs to exactly one workspace. Adding workspaceId requires refactoring all 10+ ledger method signatures; tracked separately.

Financial ledger operations are keyed by accountBalanceId (resolved through authenticated workspace-scoped calls). The ledger service API uses accountBalanceId as its primary key throughout; adding workspaceId requires a full refactor of all input types. The accountBalanceId uniquely identifies the tenant through the Connect account relationship.

Same as findUnique — ledger balance updates follow findUnique lookups keyed by accountBalanceId. The update applies only to the record just fetched. Full workspaceId propagation requires refactoring all input types.

Affiliate catalog products are fetched by IDs from a cross-workspace product catalog. Affiliate commission relationships exist between workspaces; affiliate catalog products intentionally belong to the producer workspace, not the affiliate workspace querying them.
